food_subgroup: Flatbread wraps with poultry and cheese

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for piadina or BBQ chicken piadina. FOODON_00002473 (flatbread food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for piadina specifically. A0FKP (chicken meat products) is used as the closest parent given chicken is the primary protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for BBQ chicken piadina as a composite food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One full piadina wrap (approximately 280 g) served as a complete meal, consisting of a 10-inch Italian flatbread filled with sous-vide chicken, BBQ sauce, mozzarella and cheddar cheese blend, and roasted peppers.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken burrito / flatbread wrap with cheese and sauce (composite estimation); supplemented with USDA SR Legacy entries for piadina flatbread, grilled chicken breast, BBQ sauce, and mozzarella-cheddar blend
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for a BBQ chicken piadina. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition: Italian flatbread (~35% by weight, USDA FDC #172774), grilled/sous-vide chicken breast (~35%, USDA FDC #171477), hickory BBQ sauce (~10%, USDA FDC #170187), mozzarella-cheddar cheese blend (~12%, USDA FDC #173414 and #173420), roasted red peppers (~8%, USDA FDC #170108). Values represent a blended per-100g estimate for the assembled wrap.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — wheat flatbread / piadina-type wrap with mixed fillings (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a BBQ chicken piadina. GI of 52 is estimated using the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-flour flatbread wraps with mixed protein and fat fillings (GI range 48–56 for similar products). The presence of protein (chicken), fat (cheese), and moderate sugar (BBQ sauce) lowers the effective GI relative to plain flatbread. GI 52 is the midpoint estimate; gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 65.8) / 100 = 34.2, where carbohydrate per 280g serving = 23.5 × 2.8 = 65.8g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100g; ~1456 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item as one serving provides approximately 63% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; request reduced-sauce preparation or consume only half a serving to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~34 per serving) driven by refined wheat flatbread and BBQ sauce sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with portion size as the full serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 34, which is above the low-GL threshold of 10; pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables or consuming half a wrap can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from cheese blend and moderate sodium from BBQ sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, the saturated fat contribution (~9g per serving from the mozzarella-cheddar blend) and high sodium load warrant portion control; choosing a smaller serving and requesting light cheese can reduce saturated fat and sodium to more cardioprotective levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Smokey BBQ Chicken Piadina is an Italian-inspired flatbread wrap sold at Now Pizza outlets in Singapore. It features a 10-inch light and crispy piadina — a traditional unleavened Italian flatbread originating from the Emilia-Romagna region — filled with tender sous-vide chicken breast, hickory-smoked BBQ sauce, a signature mozzarella and cheddar cheese blend, and roasted peppers. Sous-vide cooking ensures the chicken remains moist and evenly cooked at a precise low temperature before assembly. The wrap is then finished to achieve a crispy exterior. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 218 kcal, 13.5g protein, 7.8g fat (of which 3.2g is saturated), and 23.5g carbohydrate with 5.8g sugars and 1.4g dietary fibre. Sodium is notably high at approximately 520 mg per 100g, making a full serving (~280g) a significant sodium source at around 1456 mg. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full serving is approximately 34, which is moderate-to-high and relevant for blood glucose management. The cheese blend contributes calcium (~110 mg per 100g) and saturated fat. This item is suitable for general consumers seeking a protein-rich meal but requires portion awareness for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, and glycemic load profile.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central for a BBQ chicken piadina as a composite restaurant product. Nutrient values are estimated from component-weighted USDA FDC entries for flatbread, grilled chicken, BBQ sauce, cheese, and roasted peppers. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for similar wheat flatbread wraps. Confidence is medium reflecting the estimation approach rather than a direct database match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component USDA FDC source entries used for this composite estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported in the component USDA FDC entries for this composite product. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component USDA FDC entries used. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water are estimated from component USDA FDC entries and should be treated as approximate. The halal status is listed as false because it cannot be confirmed from the field survey listing alone; consumers requiring halal certification should verify directly with Now Pizza - Funan. The serving size of 280g is estimated based on a standard 10-inch piadina wrap with typical filling weights; actual restaurant serving weight may vary. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving = (52 × 65.8) / 100 = 34.2, where 65.8g carbohydrate is derived from 23.5g per 100g × 2.8 (serving factor for 280g).
